title: Green Seafood Curry

html:
GREEN SEAFOOD CURRY

Ingredients

  • 15 ml sunflower oil
  • 50 ml green curry paste (see recipe)
  • 8 spring onions, finely chopped
  • 10 ml coriander seeds, roasted
  • 10 ml cumin seeds, crushed
  • 1 ml ground cloves
  • 30 ml fresh garlic, finely chopped
  • 30 ml fresh ginger root, finely chopped
  • 2 limes’ zest, finely grated
  • 15 ml lime juice
  • 5 ml salt
  • 325 ml chicken stock
  • 1 tin coconut cream
  • 65 ml sugar
  • 600 g seafood and fish such as prawns, mussels, calamari rings, firm white fish cut into 4 cm cubes, etc – all thoroughly cleaned and any shells and heads removed
  • 250 ml baby marrow, cut into thin rings
  • 250 ml sugar snap peas (whole in the pod)
  • 250 ml tatsoi or spinach, torn small
  • 125 ml broccoli florets
  • 125 ml finely chopped parsley
  • 125 coriander leaves, roughly chopped
  • 65 ml mint leaves, finely chopped
  • 15 – 45 ml cornflour (maizena), if needed

Method

  1. Heat the oil in the pan.
  2. Fry the curry paste, spring onions, coriander seeds, cumin and cloves lightly in the oil.
  3. Add the garlic and ginger and fry for about 30 seconds or until fragrant.
  4. Now add the lime juice and zest, salt, stock, sugar and coconut cream.
  5. Let it cook down a little until it starts to thicken, or thicken with cornflour mixed with a little water until smooth.
  6. Now add the chicken, sugar snap peas and broccoli and let it cook for 4 minutes.
  7. Then add the rest of the vegetables and let it cook lightly.
  8. Add in your seafood
  9. Taste and add whatever you feel it still needs.
